story: Imagine stepping onto a cricket pitch to play for your country at just 17! That's exactly what Ian Craig did on February 6, 1953. He became the youngest Australian ever to play Test cricket, at 17 years and 239 days old. Craig's debut was a big deal, showing that age is just a number when it comes to talent. His early start in the sport inspired many young cricketers to dream big.
activity_question: How old was Ian Craig when he made his Test cricket debut?
follow_up_question: What sport do you think you'd like to be a star in at a young age?

title: Olympic Swimmer's Golden Birthday
story: Splash! A future swimming champion took his first breath. Charles Hickcox was born in Phoenix, Arizona on February 6, 1947. He grew up to become an incredible athlete in the pool. At the 1968 Olympics, Hickcox made waves by winning not one, not two, but three gold medals in medley swimming events!
activity_question: What did Charles Hickcox win at the 1968 Olympics?
follow_up_question: Do you know what 'medley' means in swimming?

title: Baseball's Home Run Hero
story: Batter up! Dale Long swung into the world on February 6, 1926, in Springfield, Missouri. This future baseball star would go on to set an amazing record. In 1956, Long hit home runs in eight games in a row! His powerful swing made him a fan favorite as he played for teams like the Pittsburgh Pirates and Chicago Cubs.
activity_question: What record did Dale Long set in 1956?
activity_choices: Hit home runs in eight consecutive games
Pitched eight no-hitters in a row
Stole eight bases in one game
Ate eight hot dogs during a game
activity_answer: Hit home runs in eight consecutive games
follow_up_question: Can you imagine how exciting it must have been for fans to watch Dale Long's home run streak?

title: A Star is Born: Cosmonaut Yuri Onufriyenko
story: Reaching for the stars began early for Yuri Ivanovich Onufriyenko. Born in Ryasne, Ukraine on February 6, 1961, he would grow up to become a celebrated Russian cosmonaut. Onufriyenko's space adventures included missions to the Mir space station and piloting the Soyuz TM-23 spacecraft. His journey from Earth to the cosmos inspired many to dream big and look up at the night sky with wonder.
activity_question: What spacecraft did Yuri Onufriyenko pilot?
follow_up_question: What do you think it feels like to be in space?

title: A Musical Genius Is Born
story: Spooky sounds and haunting melodies filled the air when Akira Yamaoka entered the world. Born on February 6, 1968, in Niigata, Japan, Yamaoka would grow up to become a famous composer. His eerie music for video games, especially the Silent Hill series, thrills players around the globe.
activity_question: Where was Akira Yamaoka born?
follow_up_question: What type of music is Akira Yamaoka famous for composing?

title: Swimming Star Makes a Splash
story: Jeff Rouse dove into the world on February 6, 1970, in Fredericksburg, Virginia. This future swimming sensation would go on to make waves in the pool. Rouse became a champion backstroke swimmer, winning gold and silver medals at the Olympic Games in 1992 and 1996. His specialty? The lightning-fast 50-meter and 100-meter backstroke races.
activity_question: What was Jeff Rouse's swimming specialty?
